title: Local Delegation Orchestration Posture
goal: Make local delegation posture explicit, human-controlled, and useful from the pre-implementation CLI path.
Frame delegation as quality-first, with token savings only when safe and not quality-compromising.
Clarify local delegation target ownership in schemas and generated reference docs.
non_goals: Do not add repo-shared CI enforcement, PR templates, or tracker-specific workflow.
Do not turn local config into a scheduler or hard-code vendor/model routing in shared repo config.
Do not auto-execute external delegation without explicit local opt-in and visible safety posture.

intent.outcome: Ship the linked local delegation issues #662, #663, and #664 as one coherent package surface.
intent.constraints: Delegation stays local-advisory unless explicitly opted into locally; human control remains visible; quality improvement outranks token savings.
intent.latitude: Choose compact field names and output shape that fit existing config/defaults/implementer-context patterns.
intent.escalation: Stop before adding a scheduler, making GitHub issue ingestion universal, or changing unrelated planning storage contracts.

execution.next_step: archive this completed plan and close the linked issues after push
execution.proof: Focused workspace CLI tests pass; schema reference is regenerated; planning surfaces remain clean.

invariants: Machine-local delegation posture must not become shared repo authority.
Delegation should improve quality where worth it or save tokens only when safe.
Pre-implementation CLI output should be compact and machine-readable.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Make agents more reliably enter the correct workflow and act as orchestrators when local posture permits it.
intent_continuity.this slice completes the larger intended: yes for the linked issues; no for full external runtime integration.

required_continuation.activation trigger: new dogfooding evidence that agents still miss execution posture or misuse local delegation mode

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: planned: local delegation control mode, implementer-context execution posture, and clearer local override docs.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: actual external executor invocation, PR/CI enforcement, and runtime-specific wrappers.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: focused tests, schema reference generation, planning checks, and dogfooding closeout.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: none unless closeout finds runtime wrapper or evidence-capture gaps

intent_interpretation.literal request: ingest and implement the three newly created delegation issues
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: move from advisory delegation posture to an actionable, human-controlled pre-implementation decision surface
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: one active execplan covering #662 through #664, implemented through config parsing, CLI output, schemas, docs, and tests
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Reject changes that make token savings override quality/proof, hide human-control posture, or route execution through GitHub-specific intake.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: src/agentic_workspace/config.py; src/agentic_workspace/cli.py; src/agentic_workspace/contracts/; docs/reference/; tests/test_workspace_cli.py; planning state and this execplan
execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "delegation or runtime or implement or config or defaults"; cd packages/planning && uv run pytest tests/test_installer.py -q; uv run python scripts/generate/generate_schema_reference.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py --quiet-success; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; make check
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: stop before changing planning storage schema, adding executor invocation, or broadening into CI/review-template enforcement
execution_bounds.stop before touching: packages/planning runtime internals, external adapter ingestion flow, or generated CLI architecture unrelated to local delegation posture

stop_conditions.stop when: the work would require actual runtime execution control instead of local advisory/suggestion output
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: human-control vocabulary cannot be represented without larger config redesign
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: implementation starts solving generic issue intake or scheduler behavior
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: focused CLI tests cannot prove config parsing and implementer-context output

context_budget.live working set: config local override parser, mixed_agent defaults/config payload, implementer context payload, local override schema/reference, focused CLI tests
context_budget.recoverable later: older runtime-posture archives and broad product thesis docs
context_budget.externalize before shift: field names, control-mode semantics, and implementer-context output contract
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Implement #662 first in config/defaults, #663 by reusing runtime_resolution in implementer context, and #664 by tightening schema descriptions.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: after config mode shape lands; before closeout; if schema generation changes many unrelated references

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: make delegation actionable but human-controlled from local posture and pre-implementation CLI output
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: local-only authority, quality-first routing, token savings only when safe, no scheduler
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: exact JSON field names, whether default mode is suggest or manual, and whether first pass includes preflight in addition to implement
delegated_judgment.escalate when: implementation would execute a target, require repo-shared local posture, or make the package decide human intent without enough task semantics

adaptive_assurance.reason: Config/schema/CLI contract changes affect shipped agent behavior but remain local-advisory and testable with focused CLI tests.

acceptance_refs: GitHub issue #662 satisfied: local delegation control mode is parsed from local config and surfaced in defaults/config output.
GitHub issue #663 satisfied: implement --changed emits execution_posture with capability posture, runtime resolution, delegation control, and handoff guidance.
GitHub issue #664 satisfied: local override schema annotations and generated reference docs clarify human-owned versus agent-managed local delegation target fields.
GitHub issue #665 satisfied: promote-to-plan creates a missing execplan for a pre-routed compact active TODO row and has a regression test.

references.label: promote-to-plan missing execplan path dogfooding fix

active_milestone.scope: Implement local delegation control mode, use runtime_resolution in implementer context, and clarify local override schema/reference docs.

immediate_next_action: Inspect config/CLI/schema/test local delegation surfaces, then implement the smallest compatible payload changes.
invariants: Local delegation posture remains local-only and advisory unless explicitly local-auto enabled.
Quality-improving escalation must be distinguishable from token-saving delegation.
Token savings cannot reduce proof, safety, or quality requirements.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: Default delegation mode should not execute targets.
Implementer context should expose an execution posture block without requiring agents to reread prose docs.
Target confidence/task-fit/capability-class fields are advisory estimates, not hard guarantees.
open_questions_to_close: Should the default local mode be suggest or manual?
What minimum task posture can implementer context derive from --task and changed paths without overclaiming?
How much ownership annotation belongs directly in the schema in the first pass?

Linked issues are closed after commit/push.
